methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Ark Robotics is a seed-stage defense and security robotics company founded by Achi Takagama. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of five same-sector and same/adjacent-stage peers, consisting of four Series A companies (Stark Defence at $500M, Vatn Systems at $300M, Ares Industries at $150M, and NODA AI at $125M) and one seed-stage peer (Tenna Systems at $60M). This peer set sits within a broader ecosystem of ten private defense-tech companies and two public comparables (DRSHF and IDEAFORGE.NS).

The peer-median method is the primary applicable triangulation approach given available data. The median valuation across the five-company peer set is $150M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This median reflects the concentration of Series A valuations in the $125M–$300M band, anchored by Ares Industries at the midpoint. The seed-stage peer (Tenna Systems at $60M) provides a floor reference, though Ark's specific operational metrics relative to this cohort remain unstated in the provided data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $128M to $172M, centered on $150M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single method and a limited set of directly comparable transactions. Key uncertainties include Ark's revenue traction, IP defensibility, and customer commitments relative to the peer set—factors that could materially shift valuation within or beyond the stated range. Additional method triangulation would require revenue multiples, discounted cash flow inputs, or transaction-level details not present in current data.
